cards.front: What is a stack data structure?
cards.back: A Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) data structure where elements are added and removed from the top

cards.front: What is the main operation performed on a stack?
What is a queue data structure?
cards.back: A First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure where elements are added to the end and removed from the front

cards.front: What is the main operation performed on a queue?
cards.back: Enqueue (add element to the end) and Dequeue (remove element from the front)

cards.front: What is the difference between a stack and a queue?
cards.back: A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, while a queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle
